content.problemStatement:
Let's call a directed graph egg-free if it is acyclic and for any three pairwise distinct vertices xxx, yyy and zzz, if the graph contains edges x→yx \rightarrow yx→y and x→zx \rightarrow zx→z, then it also contains an edge y→zy \rightarrow zy→z and/or an edge z→yz \rightarrow yz→y.

You are given an undirected graph with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges (numbered 111 through MMM). Find a way to assign a direction to each of its edges such that the resulting directed graph is egg-free, or determine that it is impossible. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: 1≤ui,vi≤N1 \le u_i, v_i \le N1≤ui​,vi​≤N for each valid iii
ui≠viu_i \neq v_iui​=vi​ for each valid iii
each pair of vertices is connected by at most one edge
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ denoting an edge between vertices uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to assign a direction to each edge in such a way that the resulting graph is egg-free, print a single line containing the string `"No solution"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, print a single line containing a string with length MMM. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string should be '^' if you want to direct the iii-th edge from uiu_iui​ to viv_ivi​, or 'v' if you want to direct it from viv_ivi​ to uiu_iui​.
